metric_value: Lucent developed first scaleable carrier-grade turnkey gateway platform for internet telephony
notes: Aberdeen's core product claim for Lucent Internet Telephony Server SP

metric_value: PC-based client requirement has alienated internet telephony from mass markets; incomplete solutions from gateway vendors
notes: Aberdeen identifies PC-client dependency as key adoption barrier being solved by Lucent

metric_value: Internet telephony promises significantly lower costs for long distance and international voice and fax traffic
notes: Aberdeen endorses internet telephony cost reduction premise

metric_value: Skype (2003) and Vonage (2004) demonstrated massive cost reductions for long distance; international VoIP calls fell to near-zero by mid-2000s; Aberdeen's prediction was fully validated
notes: Internet telephony cost reduction fully materialized; long-distance became commoditized

metric_value: Scaleable from 24 ports upward with real-time alternate routing and flexible call routing
notes: Technical specifications for scalability cited by Aberdeen

metric_value: System falls back to another data network or PSTN when data network goes down; near toll-grade sound quality
notes: Aberdeen highlights reliability as key differentiator from inferior competitors

metric_value: Lucent is first supplier capable of wide-scale implementation required for internet telephony and fax to reach mass-markets
notes: Strong Aberdeen endorsement of Lucent's first-mover advantage

metric_value: Lucent merged with Alcatel in 2006 before achieving predicted VoIP dominance; Cisco VOIP infrastructure and Sonus softswitch became more influential in carrier VoIP buildout; Lucent's specific product line was superseded
notes: Lucent did not achieve predicted first-mover dominance; Cisco and Sonus took larger share of carrier VoIP infrastructure

metric_value: elemedia SX7300P codec delivers near toll-grade sound quality through extensive call processing experience
notes: elemedia cited as key Lucent technology differentiator for voice quality

metric_value: elemedia codec was superseded by open standards (G.729 G.711 and later Opus); elemedia absorbed into Lucent; Netscape Media Server died with Netscape; Opus open-source codec now dominates
notes: Proprietary codec approach lost to open standards in VoIP market

metric_value: ISPs cable TV MSOs utilities RBOCs CAPs CLECs and IXCs all cited as potential internet telephony service providers
notes: Aberdeen maps complete service provider ecosystem as Lucent's addressable market

metric_value: All major service providers (AT&T Verizon BT Deutsche Telekom) eventually deployed IP-based voice switching; circuit-switched PSTN being phased out globally; Aberdeen's service provider VoIP prediction fully validated
notes: Every major carrier eventually migrated to IP voice infrastructure; Aberdeen's carrier adoption prediction correct

metric_value: End-users retain phones and fax machines with no behavior change; Lucent handles IP routing transparently
notes: Aberdeen correctly identifies seamless POTS compatibility as key adoption driver

metric_value: SIP (Session Initiation Protocol RFC 2543 1999) became dominant VoIP signaling standard; H.323 declined; proprietary gateway approaches like Lucent's initial implementation were superseded
notes: Standards shift to SIP changed the VoIP market architecture away from proprietary carrier-grade gateways

metric_value: Lucent targets all service provider types (ISPs cable TV MSOs utilities RBOCs CAPs CLECs IXCs) with turnkey internet telephony backed by 24x7 international support
notes: Lucent's go-to-market strategy for Internet Telephony Server SP covers full service provider spectrum

metric_value: Lucent merged with Alcatel in 2006 (Alcatel-Lucent); Nokia acquired Alcatel-Lucent in 2016 for approximately $16.6B; Lucent brand retired
notes: Lucent did not survive as independent company; Nokia is current successor

metric_value: Netscape Media Server adopted elemedia codec and echo cancellation technologies
notes: Independent vendor adoption of elemedia codec cited as market validation

metric_value: Many internet telephony technologies give poor quality of service and spotty reliability; Lucent solution delivers near toll-grade sound quality
notes: Aberdeen positions Lucent as quality leader in market characterized by poor QoS
